question_text: Which of the following properties of probability is NOT valid?
choices: If events E1 and E2 are events from the same sample space, then P(E1 + E2) = P(EI) + P(E2) - P(E1E2).
The probability of an event is always positive and less than or equal to one.
If Eo is an event which cannot occur in the sample space, the probability of Eo is zero.
If events E1 and E2 are mutually exclusive, then the probability of both events occurring is zero.
answer: If events E1 and E2 are events from the same sample space, then P(E1 + E2) = P(EI) + P(E2) - P(E1E2).

In a ballistic pendulum, a bullet of mass m is fired into a block of mass M that can swing freely. Which of the following is true for the system during

question_text: Which of the following statements is FALSE?
choices: The coefficient of restitution can be less than zero
The frictional force always acts to resist motion.
Momentum is conserved during elastic collisions.
The time rate of change of the angular momentum about a fixed point is equal to the total moment of the external forces acting on the system about the point.

Which of the following statements is FALSE?
choices: The acceleration of any point within a homogenous body rotating with a constant angular velocity is proportional to the distance of that point to the center of mass.
Kinematics is the study of the effects of motion, while kinetics is the study of the causes of motion.
The radius of gyration for a mass of uniform thickness is identical to the radius of gyration for a planar area of the same shape.
Angular momentum for rigid bodies may be regarded as the product of angular velocity and inertia.
answer: The acceleration of any point within a homogenous body rotating with a constant angular velocity is proportional to the distance of that point to the center of mass.

question_text: A charge of 0.75 C passes through a wire every 15 s. What is most nearly the current in the wire?

The rated voltage drop across a device is 50 V, and the current drawn is 30 A.
What is most nearly the power rating of this device?
The equivalent capacitance of capacitors C1 and C2 connected in series is 7.3 μF. If the capacitance of C1 = 9.6 μF, what is most nearly the capacitance of C2?
Which of the following statements regarding the motion of a conductor through a changing magnetic field is FALSE?
choices: The conductor must move at constant velocity in order to generate a current.
A current flowing counter to the direction of the conductor's motion will create a torque.
The lines of magnetic flux pass from the north pole to the south pole of the magnet.
When a conductor is "open circuited," no current flows despite its motion through the field.
answer: The conductor must move at constant velocity in order to generate a current.

question_text: Modern locomotives are powered by DC motors driven by DC generators. These are, in turn, driven by diesel engines. Which of the following is the reason for using such a configuration instead of AC generator-motor sets?
choices: The DC configuration provides high torque and good incremental power at low speeds, and performs equally well at high speeds.
The DC equipment is significantly less expensive.
Historically, the DC engine configuration has been used. There is no reason to change this.
By using the DC equipment, the power factor problems associated with AC equipment are avoided.
answer: The DC configuration provides high torque and good incremental power at low speeds, and performs equally well at high speeds.

question_text: In a DC motor, what is the definition of "field resistance"?
choices: It is the resistance of the excitation circuit.
It is the resistance of the armature windings plus the load resistance.
It is the static resistance of the motor.
It is the load resistance seen by a generator without considering inductance.
answer: It is the resistance of the excitation circuit.

question_text: In a DC motor, which of the following does NOT cause sparking at a commutator?
choices: graphite brushes with good contact pressure
no load on the output leads

question_text: The armature in a DC generator has one or more pairs of conductors or coils in which current is produced. In general, which of the following is true about the amount of power produced?
choices: Power is related only to the number of poles
Power is a function only of the output voltage and current.
No gain in power is achieved beyond four pairs of coils.

question_text: Which of the following limits the number of coils that may be placed on the armature of a DC motor or generator?
choices: coil to coil arcing due to the breakdown of insulation
the size of the load on the motor
answer: coil to coil arcing due to the breakdown of insulation

question_text:
The overall torque of a DC motor is T = KTΦIaZ
KT is a constant for the particular machine, Φ is the total magnetic flux per pole, Ia is the armature current, and Z is the number of conductors on the surface of the armature. Which of the following is FALSE regarding the above equation?
choices: It applies only to a machine having an even number of pairs of poles.
It applies since torque is indirectly dependent upon the pole winding current.
It applies whether or not there is a load on the system.
It applies to both motors and generators.
answer: It applies only to a machine having an even number of pairs of poles.

question_text: A DC system can be protected from lightning by including a thyrite tube in the circuit that connects the high-voltage line with the ground. Which of the following is FALSE regarding a thyrite tube?
choices: Its fusable link melts at high voltages.
It maintains a very high resistance at or below the system operating voltage.
Its resistance becomes low at very high voltages.
It has a very fast recovery time with regard to voltage change.
answer: Its fusable link melts at high voltages.

question_text: The magnetic saturation curve limits the voltage at which a generator or motor can operate. Which of the following statements regarding saturation curves is FALSE?
choices: Saturation does not depend upon the type of steel used in the poles.
As field current increases, the hysteresis effect limits the increase in the flux produced.
Poles that allow the production of more flux permit higher operating voltages.
More flux at a coustant field voltage can be produced by increasing the number of poles.
answer: Saturation does not depend upon the type of steel used in the poles.

question_text: Series and shunt motors are connected like series and shunt generators, respectively. The terms refer to the manner in which the self-excitation of the poles is connected to the unit. Which of the following statements is FALSE?
choices: The torque curves of both shunt and series motors are not affected by the value of the armature current.
The torque curve of a shunt motor is linear.
Field coils of the shunt motor or generator are in parallel with the armature windings.
Field coils of the series motor or generator are in series with the armature windings.
answer: The torque curves of both shunt and series motors are not affected by the value of the armature current.

question_text: In terms of efficiency, shunt and series motors or generators have similar characteristics. Which of the following statements is FALSE?
choices: The two types of motors have very similar efficiency curves.
Series motors have low torque at low speeds.
Shunt and series motors have approximately 80% efficiency above one-third of the rated load.
Efficiency decreases with lower speeds for both types of motors.
answer: The two types of motors have very similar efficiency curves.

question_text: What is pole pitch?
choices: the space on the stator allocated to one pole
the space on the stator allocated to two poles
the angle at which the pole windings are wound
the mica used to insulate the poles from each other
answer: the space on the stator allocated to one pole

question_text: Which of the following statements regarding a compound motor is FALSE?
choices: Its speed remains fairly constant when subjected to sudden loads.
It has commutators, armature windings, and field windings.
answer: Its speed remains fairly constant when subjected to sudden loads.

question_text: Which of the following does NOT contribute to core losses in DC motors?
choices: eddy currents and hysteresis losses in the armature

question_text: Which of the following are power losses in a DC motor?
Which of the following statements is FALSE regarding large DC motors?
choices: Wave winding does not pass under all poles at one time
To avoid flashover, the voltage difference between adjacent commutators should not exceed 15 V DC.
Two, four, six, or eight coils may be laid in a slot in the armature to make maximum use of the flux and to generate as much power as possible.
The pitch of the winding of a given coil is the number of slots spanned by the coil.
answer: Wave winding does not pass under all poles at one time

question_text: Which of the following statements is FALSE about the operation of parallel shunt generators?
choices: One large unit would be more expensive than the use of several smaller ones, even if it ran at full load at all times.
The drooping load or decreasing terminal voltage characteristic of shunt generators makes two or more units operating in parallel more stable.
The use of several units makes maintenance and repair easier.
The parallel configuration makes it possible to add units as needed, and to shut off unnecessary units at low load demands.
answer: One large unit would be more expensive than the use of several smaller ones, even if it ran at full load at all times.

question_text:
A generator provides power to a load dissipating 160 kW. Current flows over
760 m of two-conductor copper feeder having a resistance of 0.026 n per 100 m. The generator voltage is constant at 800 V. What is most nearly the voltage delivered at the specified load?
In a DC machine, if the field current If increases to 2If, what will the new torque be as a function of the initial torque, Tm? Neglect saturation.
An alternating current with a frequency of 60 Hz is passed through a moving coil galvanometer that measures DC current. What will the galvanometer reading be equal to?
choices: the peak value of the AC current
the average value of the AC current

Which of the following statements regarding transformers is FALSE?
choices: One three-phase transformer weighs more than three equivalent singlephase transformers.
The copper losses (I^2R) in the primary and secondary coils are equaL
Transformer power losses are generally low, approximately 1-3%.
Power losses in transformers are converted to heat, which is then dissipated.
answer: One three-phase transformer weighs more than three equivalent singlephase transformers.

question_text: An AC alternator operated as a motor is called a synchronous motor. Which of the following statements regarding synchronous motors is FALSE?
choices: The poles of a synchronous motor must be salient.
The average speed, regardless of load, does not decrease, since the motor must operate at a constant speed.
When a load is increased, the increased torque is a result of the shift in the relative positions of the fields on the rotor and stator.
The relationship between speed, frequency, and number of poles is the same for the rotating field of the induction motor and for the alternator.
answer: The poles of a synchronous motor must be salient.

question_text: Which of the following statements about induction motors is FALSE?
choices: They are used to increase the line power factor.
They have no slip rings, no brushes, and no excited field current
They have no commutators and no windings on the armature.
Squirrel-cage induction motors operate at essentially constant speeds.
answer: They are used to increase the line power factor.

question_text: A single-phase induction motor is not self-starting. Instead, auxiliary methods must be used, such as varying inductance, resistance, and capacitance. Which of the following is FALSE regarding this situation?
choices: Capacitor motors have lower starting torque than comparably sized singlephase induction motors.
To obtain a higher reactance, a capacitor can be used when starting and then be switched out of the circuit by mechanical means.
If the capacitor remains in the circuit, the power factor will have a value close to unity.
A capacitor motor uses capacitance to split the phase, resulting in two phases almost 90° apart.
answer: Capacitor motors have lower starting torque than comparably sized singlephase induction motors.

question_text: A squirrel-cage motor has such low resistance that it draws excessive currents when starting. Which of the following actions will NOT reduce this problem?
choices: connecting the windings as in a three-phase, wye, transformer, taking 58% of the normal line voltage; then, at sufficient motor speed, switching to a delta connection
using an autotransformer to reduce line voltage

question_text: Which of the following statements about AC generators is FALSE?
choices: Large turbine driven generators usually have two pole rotors to accommodate the high speed of the turbine.
The poles of an AC generator are located on the rotor.
The three main types of AC generator are direct-connect engine driven, water driven, and turbine driven.

question_text: Which of the following is FALSE regarding rotating machinery?
choices: At zero power factor, the generator delivers real power to a load.
The avoidance of harmonics in the production of a sine wave can be achieved by using a coil having multiple loops passing through adjacent slots rather than using only one pair of slots.
Uniformity in the production of flux on a pole can be obtained by using distributed field windings over a portion of the rotor surface.
AC generator ratings are usually given in units of kVA (kilovolt amps).
answer: At zero power factor, the generator delivers real power to a load.

answer: It can be determined using the simplex algorithm.

question_text: Which of the following is NOT a good application of network analysis?

Dynamic programming is a conceptual approach to problem solving.
Dynamic programming is a mathematical technique that is often useful for making a sequence of interrelated decisions.

choices: Effective rate of return in options (A), (B), and (C) is the difference between 12.55% and 12%.

$1 compounded quarterly at 3% for n yr has a future value of (1.03)^4n.
Compounding quarterly at a nominal rate of 12%/yr is equivalent to compounding annually at a rate of 12.55%.
answer: Effective rate of return in options (A), (B), and (C) is the difference between 12.55% and 12%.

question_text: A bank is advertising 9.5% accounts that yield 9.84% annually. How often is the interest compounded?
Which of the following situations has a conventional cash flow so that an internal rate of return can be safely calculated and used?
choices: You invest in a safe dividend stock and receive dividends each year.
You purchase a house and pay the bank in monthly installments.
You lease a car and pay by the month.
Your company undertakes a mining project in which the land must be reclaimed at the end of the project.
answer: You invest in a safe dividend stock and receive dividends each year.

question_text: The economic order quantity (EOQ) is defined as the order quantity that minimizes the inventory cost per unit time. Which of the following is NOT an assumption of the basic EOQ model with no shortages?
choices: There is an upper bound on the quantity ordered.
The demand rate is uniform and constant.
There is a positive cost on each unit inventoried.
The entire reorder quantity is delivered instantaneously.
answer: There is an upper bound on the quantity ordered.

question_text: Which of the following events will cause the optimal lot size, given by the classic EOQ model with no shortages, to increase?
choices: A.  a decrease in inventory carrying cost

question_text: What is a borrower of a particular loan almost always required to do during repayment?
choices: B. repay the loan over an agreed-upon amount of time
A. pay exactly the same amount of interest each payment
C. pay exactly the same amount of principal each payment
answer: B. repay the loan over an agreed-upon amount of time

question_text: What is ''work in process" classified as?
What is the indirect product cost (IPC) spending variance?
choices: the difference between actual IPC and IPC volume-adjusted budget
the IPC volume-adjusted budget (fixed+ (volume)(variable IPC rate))
the IPC volume-adjusted budget minus the total IPC absorbed
the difference between actual IPC and IPC absorbed
answer: the difference between actual IPC and IPC volume-adjusted budget

question_text:
Firm A uses full absorption costing while firm B uses variable product costing.
How will the financial statements of these companies differ?
choices: Firm A has a higher cost of goods sold, higher inventory value, and higher retained earnings.
Firm A has a smaller cost of goods sold, no change in inventory value, and no change in retained earnings
Firm A has a smaller cost of goods sold and a larger profit.
Firm A has a higher cost of goods sold and, therefore, a smaller profit
answer: Firm A has a higher cost of goods sold, higher inventory value, and higher retained earnings.

question_text: How is the material purchase price variance defined?
Which of the following does NOT affect owner's equity?
choices: expense to get license to start operation

question_text: Higrow Company is planning to grow 30% during the next fiscal year. What has to increase if Higrow is to achieve their goal?
choices: A the ratio of sales to total assets
B the ratio of total assets to equity

question_text: Z Corporation is applying for a short-term loan. In reviewing Z Corporation's financial records, the banker finds a current ratio of 2.0, an acid test ratio of 0.5, and an accounts receivable period of 70 d. What should the banker do?
choices: A. be concerned that Z Corporation will be unable to meet the payments
B. suggest that Z Corporation lower its inventories
C. suggest that Z Corporation be more aggressive in collecting on its invoices

question_text: Companies A and Bare identical except for their inventory accounting systems. Company A uses the last-in, first-out convention while company B uses the first-in, first-out convention. How will their financial statements differ in an inflationary environment?
choices: Company B's profits and inventory book value will be higher than for company A.
Company B's profits will be higher than A's, but inventory book value will be lower.
Company A's profits will be higher and the book value of their inventory will be higher than for company B.
Company A's profits will be higher and the book value of their inventory will be lower than for company B.
answer: Company B's profits and inventory book value will be higher than for company A.

question_text: What is the acid test ratio?
choices: the ratio of current assets (exclusive of inventory) to total current liabilities
the ratio of gross margin to operating, sales, and administrative expenses
the ratio of owner's equity to total current liabilities
the ratio of all assets to total liabilities
answer: the ratio of current assets (exclusive of inventory) to total current liabilities

question_text: A student needs $4000/yr for 4 yr to attend college. Her father invested $5000 in a 7% account for her education when she was born. t = 0 yr $5000

Project B also requires an investment of $1,000,000 at year zero. However, it pays $1,500,000 the next year, and nothing after that. The internal rate of return (IRR) on project A is 20%. The IRR for project B is 50%. Which is the better project? The borrowing rate is 5%
choices: Project A, because its net present worth is higher.
Project A, because it has a lower IRR.
Project B, because it has a higher IRR
answer: Project A, because its net present worth is higher.

choices: In any of its energy states, the electron moves in a circular orbit about the nucleus.
The states of allowed electron motion are those in which the angular momentum of the electron is an integral multiple of h/π.
The electron in an atom has an infinite range of motion allowed to it.

answer: In any of its energy states, the electron moves in a circular orbit about the nucleus.

The bond length for a pair of atoms is the point of lowest energy

answer: It is not possible for bonds between a pair of atoms to be different (e.g., different bond lengths or bond energies) in different compounds.

question_text: Which of the following statements is FALSE?

answer: The change in enthalpy is negative when energy is absorbed in the formation of a compound from its elements.

choices: It is greater because the shell next to it is fulL Thus, by the Pauli exclusion principle, it must jump to the first shell that is not full, in this case, the n = 3 shelL

2. The phenomenon whereby a circuit stores electrical energy is called __________.

3. A general term including material, fittings, devices, appliances, fixtures, apparatus, and the 
like used as a part of, or in connection with, an electrical installation is __________.

4. A switch intended for use in general distribution and branch circuits. It is rated in amperes, 
and it is capable of interrupting its rated current at its rated voltage, is a __________ switch.

5. The permanent joining of metallic parts to form an electrically conductive path that will ensure 
electrical continuity and the capacity to conduct safely any current likely to be imposed is known 
as __________.

6. An instrument that is used to measure the diameter of a wire or cable to thousandths of an inch 
is a __________.

7. A squirrel cage motor can be started at full voltage.
I. Design A II. Design B III. Design C IV. Design D

8. A __________ is a braking system for an electric motor.
I. Friction braking II. Plugging III. Dynamic braking

9. Rigid metal conduit is permitted for wiring in hazardous locations if the conduit is threaded 
and made up wrench tight with at least __________ full threads.

10. A circuit breaker that has purposely introduced a delay in the tripping action and which delay 
decreases as the magnitude of the current increases is a __________ circuit breaker.

11. It is the intent of this Code that factory installed internal wiring or the construction of 
equipment need not be inspected at the time of installation of the equipment, except to 
__________.
I. Detect alteration II. Detect damage III. Detect insulation type

12. A premises wiring system whose power is derived from a source such as a transformer that 
has no direct connection to the supply conductors originating in another system is a/an 
__________ system.

13. Listed or labelled equipment shall be installed, used, or both, in accordance with any 
instructions included __________.
I. By the foreman II. In the listing or labelling III. With the equipment from the manufacturer

14. Where conductors with an ampacity higher than the ampere rating or setting of the 
overcurrent device are used, the __________ shall determine the circuit rating.

15. __________ are permitted to protect motor branch circuit conductors from overload.
I. Thermal relay II. Inverse time circuit breakers III. Time delay fuses
16. The power factor of an incandescent light bulb would be __________.

17. __________ is a pliable raceway.
I. EMT II. ENT III. PVC

18. Flexible cords and cables shall be protected by __________ where passing through holes in 
covers, outlet boxes, or similar enclosures.
I. Fittings II. Bushings III. Tie wraps
19. A transformer would most likely have __________ efficiency

20. When alternating current flows through a conductor, there is an inductive action that causes 
the current in the conductor to be forced toward the outer surface. The current is greater at the 
surface than at the center of the conductor, this __________ will cause the resistance in the 
conductor to increase due to the increased heating of the conductor.

21. A value assigned to a circuit or system for the purpose of conveniently designating its 
voltage class is __________.

22. A type of AC motor that runs at a constant speed and is used for such purposes as an electric 
clock motor is a __________ motor.

23. __________ is the resistance at the point of contact of two conductors or one conductor and 
another surface.

24. __________ is/are classified as a conduit body.
I. LB fittings II. FS fittings III. LR fittings

25. __________ raceways are not suitable to enclose conductors that are subject to physical 
damage.
26. A low power factor in an industrial plant is most likely caused by __________.

27. Where lighting outlets are installed in interior stairways, there shall be a wall switch at each 
floor level to control the lighting where the difference between floor levels is __________ steps 
or more.

28. A voltage or current that is reversed at regular intervals is called __________ voltage or 
current.
I. Direct II. Steady state III. Sinusoidal
29. Of the following __________ is a false statement
choices: The power factor of a motor is much greater at partial loads than at full loads.
In an industrial plant, lower power factor is usually due to underloaded induction motors.

The term kilovolt-amperes indicate the apparent power made up of an energy component and a 
wattless or induction component.
The term kilowatt indicates the measure of power which is all available for work
answer: The power factor of a motor is much greater at partial loads than at full loads.

question_text:
30. It is generally not good practice to supply lights and motors from the same circuit because

choices: Lamps for satisfactory service must operate within closer voltage limits than motors
Overloads and short circuits are more common on motor circuits.

When motors are started, the large starting current causes a voltage drop on the circuit and the 
lights will blink or burn dim.
